title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Sore Throat
intro: Herbal teas for sore throat are a type of medicinal tea made from various herbs, plants, and flowers that help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with a sore throat. These teas work by reducing inflammation, soothing the mucous membranes, and providing antimicrobial properties that combat the underlying infection. Popular examples of herbal teas that can treat a sore throat include peppermint tea, which eases discomfort with its cooling effect; ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes digestion; chamomile tea, which calms the throat and promotes relaxation; licorice root tea, which soothes and protects the mucous membranes; and eucalyptus tea, which opens up airways and relieves congestion. By incorporating these herbal teas into one's routine, individuals can find natural relief from the pain and discomfort of a sore throat.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat sore throats due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, exhibit antioxidant and soothing effects that aid in the healing process of the throat. By reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of pathogens, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to alleviate symptoms of a sore throat, such as pain and discomfort, allowing for a faster recovery. The benefits of using these teas to treat sore throats include reduced pain and discomfort, accelerated healing, and a lower risk of developing complications, making them a popular natural remedy among individuals seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for sore throat may be effective in treating acute pharyngitis due to its strong antimicrobial activity against bacteria such as Streptococcus pyogenes, with a minimal inhibitory concentration (MIC) value of 39 μg/ml.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be an effective remedy for soothing sore throats, but it may also cause side effects such as headaches, dizziness, and an increase in blood pressure due to its high glycyrrhizin content. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume these teas in moderation, ideally for short periods, and to be mindful of individual tolerance, as excessive use may lead to potassium depletion and other complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of warm Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to soothe the throat and promote healing.
Gargle with the tea for 30 seconds to reduce throat inflammation.
Take 250-500 mg of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root 3 times a day as needed.
Continue using the tea for 5-7 days to alleviate sore throat symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat sore throats due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause sore throats, thereby alleviating symptoms and promoting healing. The benefits of ginger teas for sore throats include soothing irritation, reducing pain and discomfort, and shortening the duration of illness. Regular consumption of ginger teas has been shown to provide relief from sore throats and promote overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale (ginger) root.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add the dried ginger root to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Let the ginger root steep in the water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink as is or add honey to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pakistan journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Zingiber officinale teas for sore throat may be beneficial due to their high antioxidant activity, particularly with Zingiber officinale having the highest activity among other species of Zingiberaceae.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in treating sore throat ailments, but may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its high ginger content. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Zingiber officinale teas in large quantities, especially for pregnant women and those with bleeding disorders, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger per cup of boiling water
Take 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale tea 30 minutes before meals
Continue for 3-5 days or until sore throat subsides
Monitor and adjust dosage as needed for optimal relief

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat sore throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat sore throat by reducing inflammation, soothing the mucous membranes, and enhancing the body's immune response to fight off infections. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, such as alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids, contribute to their therapeutic effects, including their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas can provide relief from sore throat symptoms, reduce the severity of illness, and promote overall recovery, making it a popular natural remedy for this common health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Echinacea purpurea flowers and place in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into another cup.
Drink the tea hot or cold as needed for sore throat relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Echinacea purpurea teas for sore throat may be beneficial for the early treatment of acute upper respiratory infections, as eight of the nine treatment trials reported generally positive results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat a sore throat, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as hives or itching, and interactions with medications like blood thinners and diabetes medications. When using Echinacea purpurea teas, take precautions by not consuming excessive amounts, which may lead to stomach upset, and avoid using it during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to limited research on its effects on the fetus or baby.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat the process for 5-7 days to help alleviate sore throat.
Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and usage guidelines.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been widely used to treat sore throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of eucalyptus globulus teas, such as eucalyptol and cineole, help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the throat, thereby providing relief from soreness and discomfort. By acting as an expectorant, eucalyptus globulus teas help to thin and clear mucus from the throat, further alleviating symptoms of a sore throat. The benefits of using eucalyptus globulus teas to treat sore throat include natural relief from pain and inflammation, as well as the promotion of respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves, 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, or 1 Eucalyptus globulus tea bag.
Heat 1 cup of water until it boils.
Add the Eucalyptus globulus leaves or tea bag to the hot water.
Let it steep for 5-10 minutes. If using a tea bag, remove it after 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm. You can add honey to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for sore throat have been identified as one of the most effective traditional remedies in Ethiopia, with the plant species Eucalyptus globulus being among the top-cited plant species used to treat respiratory tract infections, including sore throat.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be beneficial in treating a sore throat, but it may cause gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and stomach discomfort, in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume the tea in moderation, avoid taking it with other medications, and not consume it for extended periods, as prolonged use may lead to kidney or liver damage.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Take 2-3 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey for soothing effect, if needed.
Drink tea for 2-3 days to relieve sore throat symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been used for centuries to treat sore throat ailments due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause sore throats, reducing inflammation and promoting a faster recovery. By incorporating thyme tea into one's daily routine, individuals can benefit from its natural decongestant and expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting a smoother recovery. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat sore throat ailments include reduced symptoms, faster recovery times, and a reduced reliance on over-the-counter medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water until it boils.
Add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea warm or cool, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti", Thymus vulgaris teas for sore throat can be beneficial due to its antibacterial and antiseptic effects, which can help shorten the duration of the disease and reduce the need for antibiotic therapy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be an effective remedy for sore throat, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications due to its thymol content. To use Thymus vulgaris teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses, avoid taking it for extended periods, and be cautious when combining it with other herbal preparations or medications that affect blood pressure or heart rate.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris 3 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves 3 times a day.
Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 2 hours before bed.
Continue using Thymus vulgaris tea for 3-5 days for relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been a long-standing remedy for soothing sore throats due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. The bioactive constituents present in Sambucus nigra teas, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, contribute to their therapeutic effects by fighting off infections and reducing inflammation. By consuming Sambucus nigra teas, individuals can benefit from their natural ability to soothe and heal a sore throat, providing quick relief and promoting a faster recovery.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Sambucus nigra flowers to a tea infuser.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)", Sambucus nigra teas for sore throat have been shown to significantly reduce the intensity of throat pain when swallowing and provide rapid, temporary relief of sore throat pain in patients with pharyngitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be an effective remedy for sore throats due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, but consuming excessive amounts may lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach cramps. To use Sambucus nigra teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and also to avoid taking it with other medications that may interact with its active ingredients, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times daily.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of dried flowers per cup.
Add tea to warm water and stir well.
Drink tea 15-30 minutes after meals for best results.
Continue using tea for 2-5 days to soothe sore throat.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat sore throats due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation, combat bacterial and viral infections, and promote healing. Drinking yarrow tea may help to alleviate a sore throat by reducing swelling, relieving pain, and promoting a soothing effect on the throat. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat a sore throat include reduced inflammation, accelerated recovery, and a natural alternative to pharmaceutical medications, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to healthcare.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. You can use dried ones if fresh is not available.
Measure 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add 2 tablespoons of the gathered leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down. Drink it warm or cold, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can provide relief from sore throat symptoms, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or digestive issues, in some individuals. When using Achillea millefolium teas to treat sore throat, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consumption if you have any known allergies or sensitivities, as well as to be cautious when using it for an extended period of time.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Achillea millefolium tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Achillea millefolium leaves 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 cup of tea or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves per serving.
Drink tea or take dried leaves for 3-5 days for sore throat relief.
Repeat as needed but not more than 7 days consecutively.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat sore throats due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, such as quinine and alkaloids, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause sore throats. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Cinchona officinalis teas help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with a sore throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat a sore throat include reduced pain and inflammation, accelerated healing, and a natural, non-addictive alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, a cup of boiling water, and a cup for the tea.
Place the dried Cinchona bark in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Pour the boiling water over the Cinchona bark and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into the cup and discard the Cinchona bark.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and drink the tea while it's warm to soothe a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Cinchona officinalis teas are not mentioned in the study as a treatment for sore throat, however the study mentions that a large number of plant species were reported to treat coughs, colds, dysentery, dampness and sore throats, but the specific species used for sore throat were not identified.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be an effective treatment for sore throats, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to their high quinine content. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, including avoiding them in pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as people with conditions such as kidney or liver disease, and those taking certain medications like blood thinners.

preparations.herb_desc: Althaea officinalis teas, derived from the root of the marshmallow plant, have been traditionally used to treat sore throats due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing inflammation and discomfort in the throat, thereby alleviating the symptoms of a sore throat. The bioactive constituents, such as mucilages (heteropolysaccharides), present in Althaea officinalis teas, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, providing relief from pain and discomfort. The benefits of using Althaea officinalis teas to treat a sore throat include its natural and non-invasive approach, making it a popular choice among those seeking a holistic remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Althaea officinalis root.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Althaea officinalis root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink while it is warm, up to 3 times a day.

The saponins and phenolic acids present in Silybum marianum teas have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which help to inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms that can contribute to sore throat. Drinking Silybum marianum teas regularly can provide numerous benefits, including reducing the severity and duration of sore throat symptoms, promoting healing, and preventing future occurrences.

Steep 1 tablespoon of the chopped Silybum marianum in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into another cup, discard the solids, and let it cool down.
Drink the tea when it's lukewarm or at room temperature, up to 3 times a day as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat sore throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthone, menthyl acetate, and limonene, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections in the throat, thereby alleviating symptoms of sore throat. Drinking peppermint tea can help to soothe the throat, reduce pain and discomfort, and promote a sense of calm, which can aid in the recovery process. The benefits of using peppermint tea to treat sore throat ailments include its natural and non-addictive properties, making it a safe and effective alternative to pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and 1 teaspoon of lemon juice (optional) into a cup.
Combine the peppermint leaves and boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into the cup with the honey and lemon juice (if using). Discard the peppermint leaves.
Drink the tea while it's warm to help soothe a sore throat.
